name: clawket
description: Structured task contracts for LLM coding agents — Claude Code plugin with local SQLite-backed task board + MCP RAG pull across sessions

skillsList.name: clawket-dashboard
skillsList.description: Use when checking work status, starting or finishing tasks, or creating tasks in the Clawket work board. Shows the current plan / unit / cycle / task view for the working directory and routes start / done / new sub-commands through the lifecycle gates.

skillsList.name: clawket-plan-design
skillsList.description: Use when designing a Clawket Plan + Units from authored scenarios — pins the Done proposition, decomposes scenarios into Units, declares the Unit dependency graph, and sets the convergence condition before any work begins.

skillsList.name: clawket-scenario-author
skillsList.description: Use when authoring atomic user scenarios for a new domain or menu area — produces per-domain spec knowledge in the strict As-a / I-want / So-that + Given/When/Then format.

skillsList.name: clawket-verify-batch
skillsList.description: Use during a verification round to dispatch sub-agent batches that reason scenarios against code, emit per-batch TSV evidence, then transcribe the TSV into Clawket tasks via a 16-worker ThreadPoolExecutor.

skillsList.name: clawket-verify-loop
skillsList.description: Use to run a verification round end-to-end — dispatch sub-agent batches, collect 7-field TSV evidence, transcribe into Clawket tasks, render the 3-way convergence judgment, and either schedule the next round or close the loop.

skillsList.name: clawket-scenario-refine
skillsList.description: Use when a verification round reports scenario_error rows — decide atomic-split / intent-redefinition / deletion for each scenario, then update the spec knowledge and the audit trail.

skillsList.name: clawket-defect-fix
skillsList.description: Use when a verification round reports defect rows — register a fix task per defect under the defect-resolution plan's Round R Unit, apply the code change there (never in the QA plan), and verify the Done proposition is externally checkable.
